confidenceRationale: Ownership is documented in an unbroken chain from the artist through his brother Theo van Gogh, Theo's widow Johanna van Gogh-Bonger, their son V.W. van Gogh, and dealer Paul Rosenberg, to the Museum of Modern Art's 1941 acquisition — corroborated by MoMA's own catalogue record, Wikipedia, and Wikidata. No custody gaps and no watchlist or loss-registry matches were found.

provenanceTimeline.note: Painted from memory and imagination while a patient at the Saint-Paul-de-Mausole asylum in Saint-Rémy-de-Provence, France.
Sent by the artist to his brother and dealer, Theo van Gogh, in Paris shortly after completion.

provenanceTimeline.owner: Johanna van Gogh-Bonger, Paris and Amsterdam
provenanceTimeline.note: Inherited by Theo's widow after his death in January 1891; Johanna was chiefly responsible for building Vincent van Gogh's posthumous reputation.

provenanceTimeline.owner: Vincent Willem van Gogh (V.W. van Gogh), Laren, Netherlands
provenanceTimeline.note: Passed to Theo and Johanna's son, the artist's nephew, upon Johanna's death in 1925.

provenanceTimeline.owner: Paul Rosenberg, Paris and New York
provenanceTimeline.note: Acquired by the Paris dealer Paul Rosenberg, who relocated the gallery's most important stock to New York shortly before the German invasion of France.

provenanceTimeline.owner: The Museum of Modern Art, New York
provenanceTimeline.note: Acquired in 1941 through the Lillie P. Bliss Bequest, where it remains one of the museum's signature works.

riskFlags.detail: The work passed through Paris dealer Paul Rosenberg's hands in 1938–1941, immediately before the German occupation of France. Rosenberg's own collection was later looted by the Nazis, but this specific painting had already been sold to MoMA in 1941 and does not appear on any loss registry.

valuationAssessment.note: No sale price applies; the work entered MoMA's collection via bequest, not purchase.

authoritiesConsulted.name: Tavily — provenance & looting research
authoritiesConsulted.domain: metmuseum.org, getty.edu, interpol.int, unesco.org, artloss.com, lostart.de, lootedart.com, christies.com, sothebys.com, artnet.com, fbi.gov, ifar.org, wikipedia.org

passportSignature.attestation: This passport records the results of automated queries to free, public provenance and risk-screening sources, captured as a static snapshot for offline/demo use. It attests to process, not to underlying truth.
